_note: Original, owned NET news content in the era of Count Binary — our prose.

news.headline: Maas Biolabs Confirms 'Minor Thermal Event' at Arizona Facility
news.body: Satellite imagery showing a two-kilometer glass scar in the desert was described by a Maas spokesman as 'consistent with routine operations.' Analysts note the company's hybridoma division has stopped returning calls, and its star researcher has not been seen at conference since spring.

news.headline: Virek Estate Denies Health Rumors for Eleventh Consecutive Year
news.body: Josef Virek, believed to be the wealthiest private individual alive, remains 'in excellent spirits and complete legal existence,' per his Stockholm counsel. The billionaire has not been photographed in the flesh since the vat was installed. His art-market activity, however, has recently become 'energetic.'

news.headline: Gothick-Kasual Violence Spikes in Barrytown; Nobody Knows Why They Stopped
news.body: After a week of coordinated street action that police describe as 'unprecedentedly well-funded,' rival youth gangs abruptly dispersed Tuesday night. Asked who had been paying them, one Gothick elder statesman said: 'Money stopped. Hate's free but it don't buy bus fare.'

news.headline: Residential Explosion in Covina Concourse Courts Ruled 'Gas'
news.body: The sixteenth-floor blast that destroyed a single condominium unit has been attributed to a fault no inspector can locate, in a building with no gas service. The unit's occupant, a hairdresser, was not home. Her son could not be reached.

news.headline: Sense/Net Scouts 'New Face' Amid Tally Isham Contract Talks
news.body: The simstim giant is quietly auditioning unknowns as negotiations with its veteran star enter a testy decade. An executive, speaking anonymously, said the network seeks 'someone the cameras love and the future has already met.'

news.headline: Hounfor Attendance Up Sharply in the Projects
news.body: Community leaders report a surge of interest in the old religions, particularly among console operators. 'The loa ride the wire now,' one mambo told this service, declining further comment. Corporate AI-affairs desks continue to classify such reports as folklore.

news.headline: Orbital Salvage Claim Filed on Derelict Tessier-Ashpool Cores
news.body: An independent tug operating out of the Freeside archipelago has registered a working claim on the derelict corporate cores at the archipelago's trailing edge. The famously reclusive T-A estate, in liquidation for years, filed no objection. Nobody ever does.
